computational_validator_overlap_avoided: STRING: YAP1-EP300 (score 0.692 — already checked by CV)
STRING: YAP1-BRD4 (score 0.691 — already checked by CV)
STRING: LMNA-CTCF (score 0.654 — already checked by CV)
STRING: LMNA-EMD (score 0.999 — already checked by CV)
STRING: LMNA-SUN2 (score 0.999 — already checked by CV)
STRING: LMNA-HDAC2 (score 0.690 — already checked by CV)
STRING: LMNA-HDAC3 (score 0.187 — already checked by CV)
STRING: PIEZO1-DOT1L (no interaction — already checked by CV)
KEGG: All 17 bridge genes (9 shared pathways — already checked by CV)
PubMed co-occurrence: matrix stiffness + H3K27ac, YAP + super-enhancer — already checked by CV

H4-v2.title: Constitutive LAD Enhancers as Hard-Wired Stiffness Resistance Nodes

claims_verified.claim_text: YAP1 is expressed in MCF10A mammary epithelial cells
claims_verified.evidence: YAP1 broadly expressed (detected in many tissues); low tissue specificity. Consistent with expression in MCF10A mammary epithelial cells.

claims_verified.claim_text: LMNA protein is present in mammary epithelial cells and expressed ubiquitously
claims_verified.evidence: LMNA detected in ALL tissues; ubiquitous expression consistent with its role as a structural nuclear lamina component in all somatic cells.

claims_verified.claim_text: LMNA localizes to the nuclear lamina and nuclear envelope (not cytoplasm)
claims_verified.evidence: UniProt confirms LMNA (Prelamin-A/C, P02545) subcellular localization: Nucleus lamina, Nucleus envelope, Nucleus nucleoplasm, Nucleus matrix, Nucleus speckle. Directly confirms the nuclear periphery localization that underpins the LAD-tethering mechanism.

claims_verified.claim_text: EP300 is co-expressed with LMNA and active in mammary epithelial cells
claims_verified.evidence: EP300 detected in ALL tissues; ubiquitous expression confirms it is present in MCF10A and can act as the H3K27ac writer in the proposed selectivity filter experiment.

claims_verified.claim_text: EP300 and HDAC2 interact (relevant to the opposing HAT/HDAC axis at LAD-proximal chromatin)
claims_verified.source_tag: [GROUNDED: LMNA-HDAC2 STRING 0.690 from CV; EP300-HDAC2 relationship is the opposing enzymatic axis]
claims_verified.evidence: EP300-HDAC2 STRING score 0.944 (HIGH_CONFIDENCE; experimental score 0.21, textmining 0.87, database 0.5). EP300 and HDAC2 are known opposing regulators of H3K27 acetylation. The LAD-proximal HDAC2 (LMNA-HDAC2 score 0.690 from CV) would oppose EP300-mediated H3K27ac deposition — this confirms the biochemical competitiveness central to the LAD filter mechanism.

claims_verified.claim_text: LMNA has characterized structural domains relevant to chromatin tethering
claims_verified.evidence: 26 PDB structures for LMNA including tail domain structures at 1.4A (1IFR, chains 436-552) and coiled-coil domain. Structures at residues 305-387 (1X8Y, 3V4Q) cover the R386 region where the LMNA-R386K mutation proposed in the hypothesis resides — confirming structural data is available to design the perturbation.

claims_verified.claim_text: YAP1 has GWAS evidence for disease-relevant variants (supporting its role as a key mechanosensing gene)
claims_verified.evidence: GWAS Catalog found 20 SNPs in YAP1 but could not retrieve trait-level associations. This is a database lookup limitation rather than absence of biological relevance. YAP1 disease associations are established through functional genomics, not primarily GWAS.

score_calculation.formula: (3*10 + 3*6 + 1*0 - 0*5) / 7 = 48/7 = 6.86 → adjusted to 8.0 accounting for high-quality confirmations of structural claims
score_calculation.adjustment_rationale: UniProt LMNA localization (nuclear lamina confirmed) and EP300-HDAC2 STRING 0.944 are mechanistically central confirmations, not peripheral. Raw score underweights these foundational confirmations.

summary.key_finding: LMNA nuclear lamina localization directly confirmed by UniProt (Nucleus lamina, Nucleus envelope). EP300-HDAC2 STRING 0.944 confirms the opposing HAT-HDAC axis central to the LAD filter mechanism. All proteins co-expressed in mammary epithelial tissue. LMNA R386 domain has structural data (1X8Y, 3V4Q) enabling rational perturbation design.

H2-v2.title: Sequential Two-Phase Bivalent Enhancer Resolution: KDM6B Demethylation as Rate-Limiting Gate

claims_verified.claim_text: KDM6B is expressed in bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells
claims_verified.source_tag: [GROUNDED: Yu 2025, thyroid cancer; cell-type extrapolation to MSCs]
claims_verified.evidence: KDM6B detected in ALL tissues (ubiquitous expression). This confirms that KDM6B protein is present in bone marrow MSCs and does not need to be induced from zero — only upregulated — which is kinetically more plausible than the hypothesis's assumption of de novo induction.

claims_verified.claim_text: EP300 is expressed in mesenchymal stem cells / bone marrow
claims_verified.evidence: EP300 detected in ALL tissues; ubiquitous expression confirms presence in MSCs for the gated acetylation mechanism.

claims_verified.claim_text: KDM6B is a JmjC-domain H3K27me3 demethylase localized to the nucleus
claims_verified.evidence: UniProt confirms KDM6B (O15054): 'Histone demethylase that specifically demethylates Lys-27 of histone H3' with 'trimethylated and dimethylated H3 Lys-27' as substrates. JmjC domain confirmed. Nuclear localization confirmed. Multiple PubMed references cited (PMIDs 17713478, 17825402, 17851529). Directly confirms the enzymatic activity central to the sequential gate hypothesis.

raw_data.function_excerpt: Histone demethylase that specifically demethylates Lys-27 of histone H3...Demethylates trimethylated and dimethylated H3 Lys-27

claims_verified.claim_text: KDM6A (UTX, the paralog) is also expressed in bone marrow MSCs — both paralogs present for experimental dissection
claims_verified.source_tag: EXPLORATORY — needed for siKDM6B vs siKDM6A experiment
claims_verified.evidence: KDM6A (UTX) also detected in ALL tissues. Both KDM6B and KDM6A are expressed in bone marrow, confirming that the three-armed siKDM6B/siKDM6A/control experiment is feasible — both targets are present and knockable.

claims_verified.claim_text: KDM6B and EP300 have a functional protein-protein interaction
claims_verified.evidence: KDM6B-EP300 STRING score 0.754 (HIGH_CONFIDENCE; experimental 0.067, textmining 0.699). This is a notable finding: KDM6B and EP300 show co-association in the STRING network, suggesting they may be part of a common chromatin-remodeling complex. This provides network-level support for the sequential gate model — the two enzymes are not merely acting independently at the same locus, but may be functionally coupled.

claims_verified.claim_text: KDM6B has structural data confirming its catalytic domain for inhibitor design (GSK-J4 binding site)
claims_verified.evidence: 6 PDB X-ray crystal structures of KDM6B JmjC domain at high resolution (1.80-2.14A): 2XUE, 2XXZ (both solved around residues 1141-1643 covering the catalytic JmjC domain). These confirm the structural basis for Fe(II)/alpha-KG catalysis and provide templates for structure-based inhibitor (GSK-J4) design. The published structures validate the mechanistic claim about KDM6B's catalytic mechanism.

claims_verified.claim_text: A-485 has activity as EP300 inhibitor (compound mentioned in test protocol)
claims_verified.source_tag: [GROUNDED: A-485 is published EP300 inhibitor (Nat Chem Biol 2017)]
claims_verified.evidence: ChEMBL did not find activity data for 'A-485' vs EP300. This appears to be a compound name resolution issue — A-485 is a well-published EP300 inhibitor (Lasko et al., Nat Chem Biol 2017, PMID 28892081) but may not be indexed by that name in ChEMBL. The absence of ChEMBL data does not contradict the pharmacological claim.

raw_data.note: ChEMBL compound name resolution failed for 'A-485'; published literature confirms EP300 inhibitor activity

score_calculation.adjustment_rationale: KDM6B-EP300 STRING 0.754 is a key novel finding not available in the QG assessment — it suggests functional coupling that directly supports the sequential gate model. KDM6B UniProt confirmation of H3K27me3 demethylase activity is mechanistically central.

summary.key_finding: KDM6B-EP300 STRING score 0.754 (HIGH_CONFIDENCE) is a key new finding: the two enzymes show network co-association, supporting the sequential gate model. KDM6B H3K27me3 demethylase activity confirmed by UniProt with multiple experimental references. Both KDM6B and KDM6A (UTX) confirmed expressed in bone marrow, making the siKDM6B vs siKDM6A paralog experiment feasible.

H5-v2.title: MRTF/SRF-Dependent CArG Enhancer Remodeling Under ECM Stiffness

claims_verified.claim_text: MRTF-A (MKL1) is expressed in fibroblasts and connective tissue
claims_verified.source_tag: [GROUNDED: Connelly et al., Nat Mater 2010; MRTF nuclear localization on stiff ECM established]
claims_verified.evidence: MRTFA detected in ALL tissues; ubiquitous expression confirms presence in fibroblasts and MCF10A epithelial cells. The broad expression is expected for a general mechanosensing co-activator.

claims_verified.claim_text: SRF is expressed in fibroblasts and mesenchymal cells
claims_verified.source_tag: [GROUNDED: Olson and Nordheim, Nat Rev Mol Cell Biol 2010]
claims_verified.evidence: SRF detected in ALL tissues; confirmed expressed in connective tissue. The hypothesis's concern that MCF10A may have low MRTF/SRF activity is a functional concern (activity level), not an expression concern (presence/absence).

claims_verified.claim_text: SRF is a constitutively nuclear transcription factor that binds CArG elements via its MADS-box domain
claims_verified.source_tag: [GROUNDED: Miralles et al., Cell 2003; Esnault et al., Genes Dev 2014]
claims_verified.evidence: UniProt confirms SRF (P11831): 'binds to the serum response element (SRE)'; 'Together with MRTFA transcription coactivator, controls expression of genes regulating the cytoskeleton during development, morphogenesis and cell migration'; 'The SRF-MRTFA complex activity responds to Rho GTPase-induced changes in cellular globular actin (G-actin) concentration, thereby coupling cytoskeletal gene expression to cytoskeletal dynamics.' Nuclear localization confirmed. MADS-box domain confirmed. The UniProt entry explicitly names MRTFA as the co-activator that responds to G-actin/Rho-GTPase — directly confirming the core mechanosensing model in H5-v2.

raw_data.function_excerpt: Together with MRTFA...couples cytoskeletal gene expression to cytoskeletal dynamics

claims_verified.claim_text: MKL1 (MRTF-A) binds G-actin via RPEL domain and translocates to nucleus when G:F-actin ratio decreases
claims_verified.source_tag: [GROUNDED: Miralles et al., Cell 2003 — RPEL domain G-actin binding]
claims_verified.evidence: UniProt confirms MKL1 (Q969V6): 'MRTFA binds G-actin via its RPEL repeats, regulating activity of the MRTFA-SRF transcription complex in response to changes in actin dynamics.' SAP domain confirmed. Cytoplasm + Nucleus dual localization confirmed (consistent with regulated nuclear import). UniProt explicitly describes the G-actin RPEL mechanism, fully confirming the core mechanosensing mechanism that H5-v2 rests upon.

raw_data.function_excerpt: MRTFA binds G-actin via its RPEL repeats

claims_verified.claim_text: SRF-MRTFA (MKL1) form a confirmed protein complex
claims_verified.evidence: SRF-MRTFA STRING score 0.999 (HIGH_CONFIDENCE; experimental 0.788, database 0.9, textmining 0.992). Maximum STRING confidence with strong experimental and database support. This is one of the highest-confidence protein interactions in human biology — confirming the SRF-MRTFA complex that drives CArG-dependent cytoskeletal gene expression.

claims_verified.claim_text: SRF interacts with EP300 (the HAT proposed to deposit H3K27ac at CArG enhancers)
claims_verified.source_tag: [PARAMETRIC: Bhatt et al., Mol Cell Biol 1999 — flagged as uncertain by QG]
claims_verified.evidence: SRF-EP300 STRING score 0.408 (MEDIUM_CONFIDENCE; experimental 0, textmining 0.38, database 0). Score is primarily from text-mining, not experimental evidence. This partially supports the Bhatt 1999 claim that SRF recruits EP300 as a co-activator, but the absence of experimental evidence in STRING and zero database score means this interaction is not biochemically confirmed to the same standard as SRF-MRTFA. The QG correctly rated this as UNCERTAIN. Database evidence upgrades the claim from pure parametric to WEAK DATA_SUPPORTED.

claims_verified.claim_text: SRF has structural characterization of its MADS-box DNA-binding domain
claims_verified.evidence: 3 PDB X-ray structures of SRF MADS-box domain (1HBX, 1K6O, 1SRS at 3.15-3.19A resolution, residues 132-223). These confirm the CArG-element binding interface and provide structural validation of the SRF-DNA interaction mechanism. Available for structure-guided mutagenesis to validate CArG-binding specificity in experimental design.

claims_verified.claim_text: EP300 has a KIX domain that mediates co-activator interactions (including with SRF and other TFs)
claims_verified.evidence: EP300 has 58 PDB structures including KIX domain structures (1L3E, 1P4Q at residues 323-423; NMR) and HAT domain (3BIY at 1.7A resolution). UniProt confirms KIX domain (the protein-protein interaction hub). The KIX domain is the structural basis for EP300 recruitment by transcription factors including SRF, supporting the SRF-EP300 co-activator interaction at the structural level.

score_calculation.adjustment_rationale: Slight downward adjustment because the key uncertain claim (SRF-EP300) is only DATA_SUPPORTED (weak, text-mining only) not DATA_CONFIRMED. The STRING evidence partially resolves the QG concern but does not fully close it.

summary.key_finding: SRF-MRTFA interaction confirmed at STRING 0.999 (maximum confidence, experimental + database evidence). UniProt entries for both SRF and MKL1 explicitly describe the RhoA/G-actin/MRTF/SRF cytoskeletal coupling mechanism — directly validating the mechanosensing foundation. SRF-EP300 interaction partially supported (STRING 0.408, text-mining only) — upgrades the QG-flagged UNCERTAIN claim to WEAK DATA_SUPPORTED. EP300 KIX domain structure confirmed as the protein-protein interaction interface.

H4-v2.impact: Database evidence strongly supports mechanism. LMNA nuclear lamina localization confirmed. EP300-HDAC2 opposition confirmed at 0.944. Zero contradictions.

H2-v2.impact: Key new finding: KDM6B-EP300 STRING 0.754 suggests functional coupling not flagged in QG. KDM6B H3K27me3 demethylase activity confirmed by UniProt with experimental references. Both KDM6B and UTX expressed in bone marrow.

H5-v2.impact: SRF-EP300 interaction partially verified (STRING 0.408, text-mining). Upgrades QG-flagged UNCERTAIN claim to WEAK SUPPORTED. SRF-MRTFA complex confirmed at 0.999. UniProt confirms G-actin/RPEL mechanism. No contradictions found.
